content.content:
Web search results for query: "CSS masonry "display: grid-lanes" OR "display: masonry" CSSWG resolution 2025 final decision"

Links: [{"title":"Masonry layout - CSS - MDN Web Docs","url":"https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/Guides/Grid_layout/Masonry_layout"},{"title":"Masonry Layout is Now grid-lanes | CSS-Tricks","url":"https://css-tricks.com/masonry-layout-is-now-grid-lanes/"},{"title":"Brick by brick: Help us build CSS Masonry | Blog | Chrome for Developers","url":"https://developer.chrome.com/blog/masonry-update"},{"title":"Masonry Layout is Now grid-lanes","url":"https://247webdevs.blogspot.com/2025/12/masonry-layout-is-now-grid-lanes.html"},{"title":"Help us choose the final syntax for Masonry in CSS | WebKit","url":"https://webkit.org/blog/16026/css-masonry-syntax/"},{"title":"Creating a Responsive Pure CSS Masonry Layout - W3Bits","url":"https://w3bits.com/blog/css-masonry/"},{"title":"CSS WG Blog – CSS Grid: Level 1 & 2 updated, Level 3 (masonry layout) reworked","url":"https://www.w3.org/blog/CSS/2025/03/28/css-grid-1-2-3-updated/"},{"title":"Masonry Layout is Now grid-lanes","url":"https://cpanelplus.net/masonry-layout-is-now-grid-lanes/"},{"title":"Masonry layout - CSS | MDN","url":"https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/CSS_grid_layout/Masonry_layout"}]

Based on the search results, here's what I found regarding the CSSWG resolution for CSS masonry syntax:

## CSSWG Final Decision on Masonry Syntax

In a meeting held on January 31st, 2025, the CSS Working Group resolved to reuse grid templating and placement properties for masonry layout.

The decision was that setting `grid-lanes` on the display property activates a masonry layout, and it's going to be `display: grid-lanes`.

## Implementation Details

Level 3 of the CSS grid layout specification defines masonry layout (also known as grid-lanes layout), which is accessed via the display values `grid-lanes` and `inline-grid-lanes`.

To create the most common masonry layout where the columns are laid out in a grid and the rows stack like masonry, use `display: grid-lanes` along with `grid-template-columns`, and the child elements will lay out item by item along the stacking axis according to the masonry algorithm.

## Browser Support

The Firefox-only `grid-template-rows: masonry` approach was dropped by CSSWG in favour of CSS Grid Lanes, which are supported in Safari 26.4+ and also available as an experimental feature in Chrome and Firefox.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.
